title: Concept
description: A theological concept or doctrine. One file per concept. This schema is the contract for the public API payload; every field here is served as-is.

id.description: Stable slug. Becomes part of the API URL. Never change once published.

tradition.description: Religious/theological tradition. Starts with 'islam'; enum will grow as scope widens.

primary.description: Common romanized name used as the display label.

original.description: Name in its original script (e.g. Arabic).

category.description: Theological subfield. Free-form for now; may become an enum once the taxonomy stabilizes.

summary.description: One to two neutral sentences. Must not favor any interpretive school.

description.description: Longer neutral exposition. Claims should be traceable to entries in 'sources'. Keep to a few paragraphs; long-form essays belong to a future 'article' entity.

editorial_notes.description: Internal notes for editors (why a source was chosen, open questions, disputes). Served publicly for transparency — write it to be read.

relations.description: Typed links to other entities. This is what turns the dataset into a graph.

target.description: id of the target entity (may not exist yet).

historical_period.description: Rough temporal anchor. Years may be approximate; use 'note' to qualify.

start.description: Year (CE, negative for BCE). Approximate is fine.

sources.description: Provenance. The heart of the project's credibility. Every substantive claim should rest on one of these.

license.description: License / usage terms of the source, if known.

confidence.description: Editor's confidence that this source supports the claims it backs.

status.description: Editorial lifecycle. Only 'reviewed' and 'verified' are meant to be relied on.

schema_version.description: SemVer of the schema this file was written against.
